architecture.summary: Weakly Supervised Neuro-Symbolic Module Network (WNSMN) for numerical reasoning over text. The system executes a noisy heuristic program derived from dependency parsing of the query as discrete actions over both neural and symbolic reasoning modules. Information flows from question text through dependency parsing into a sequence of discrete module operations (arithmetic, comparison, filtering over passage spans and numerical values), trained end-to-end via reinforcement learning with answer-matching reward signal, producing a final numerical answer.
trained_components: module selection and argument binding policies

biblio.title: Weakly Supervised Neuro-Symbolic Module Networks for Numerical Reasoning over Text
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: WNSMN outperforms NMN by 32% in exact match accuracy on the subset of DROP having numerical answers under comparable weakly supervised settings
WNSMN outperforms the reasoning-free generative language model GenBERT by 8% in exact match accuracy under comparable weakly supervised settings
WNSMN learns to execute a noisy heuristic program obtained from the dependency parse of the query, as discrete actions over both neural and symbolic reasoning modules
WNSMN is trained with answers as the sole supervision for numerical reasoning based MRC

Contemporary Neural Module Networks models fail to generalize to more open-ended settings without strong supervision in form of specialized program annotation from the QA pairs through various heuristic parsing and exhaustive computation of all possible discrete operations on discrete arguments
